opendb_id.description: Unique identifier for the document (UUID v4)

min_fan_rpm.description: Min value of the fan's rotational speed in RPM. If constant, fill in min_fan_rpm and ignore max_fan_rpm

max_fan_rpm.description: Max value of the fan's rotational speed in RPM. If constant, fill in min_fan_rpm and ignore max_fan_rpm

min_noise_level.description: The min noise level of the cooler in dB. If constant, fill in min_noise_level and ignore max_noise_level

max_noise_level.description: The max noise level of the cooler in dB. If constant, fill in min_noise_level and ignore max_noise_level

color.description: The color(s) of this product. Each color of the product should be included in the array. Includes heatsink or radiator assembly colors

height.description: The height of the CPU cooler in mm

cpu_sockets.description: An array of CPU socket types supported by the cooler (e.g., AM4, AM5, LGA 1700, LGA 1200)

water_cooled.description: Indicates if the cooler is water-cooled

radiator_size.description:
Radiator size in mm (e.g. 360, 280, 240).

Populate this for water-cooled models.

fanless.description: Whether the cooler can operate fanless

fan_size.description: Size of the included fan(s) in mm (e.g. 140, 120)

general_product_information.description: General product information and retailer SKUs

series.description:
Product series name

The series is the manufacturer's marketed product line / family for the cooler. Manufacturer (e.g., NZXT, Corsair, Arctic, Noctua, be quiet!, Thermalright, DeepCool) and water_cooled (true=Liquid, false=Air) are stored separately and form the default grouping, so do NOT repeat them in the series. Radiator size (`radiator_size`: 240, 280, 360, 420), fan size (`fan_size`: 120, 140), color (`manufacturer_color`), and fan count are all separate fields and MUST NOT be in the series. Liquid examples: 'NZXT Kraken Plus 360mm Black (2025)' → series='Kraken Plus'. 'NZXT Kraken Elite 280mm RGB White' → series='Kraken Elite'. 'Corsair iCUE H150i Elite Capellix XT' → series='iCUE Elite Capellix XT' (use the family name; the H150i / H100i SKU prefix encodes the radiator size, which already lives in `radiator_size`). 'Arctic Liquid Freezer III Pro 360 Black' → series='Liquid Freezer III Pro'. Air examples: 'Noctua NH-D15 chromax.black' → series='NH-D15'. 'Noctua NH-D15 G2' → series='NH-D15 G2' (G2 is the next-gen product line, not a variant of NH-D15). 'Noctua NH-U12A' → series='NH-U12A'. 'be quiet! Dark Rock Pro 5' → series='Dark Rock Pro 5'. 'Thermalright Peerless Assassin 120 SE' → series='Peerless Assassin 120 SE' (the 120 here is part of the marketed family name, not a fan_size selector — but if a manufacturer ships the same family in 120 and 140 spreads, drop the size from the series and rely on `fan_size`). The series is what stays consistent across radiator-size / color SKUs of the same product line.

variant.description:
Specific variant in the series

Most coolers should have an empty variant. Radiator size lives in `radiator_size`, fan size in `fan_size`, fan count in `fan_quantity`, color in `manufacturer_color`. Only fill `variant` when the manufacturer ships a genuinely distinct sub-model under the same series name that needs its own selector (different cold-plate, different included display module, regional / OEM-only spin, named anodized finish kit, etc.). Liquid examples: 'NZXT Kraken Plus 240mm Black' / 'NZXT Kraken Plus 280mm Black' / 'NZXT Kraken Plus 360mm Black' → variant='' for all three (only `radiator_size` differs — do NOT put '240mm Black' / '280mm Black' / '360mm Black' in variant). 'NZXT Kraken Elite 360 RGB White' → variant='' (RGB / White live in their own fields; the LCD is part of the Elite series itself). 'Corsair iCUE H150i Elite Capellix XT' (only one SKU per radiator size) → variant=''. Air examples: 'Noctua NH-D15 chromax.black' → variant='chromax.black' (chromax.black is a manufacturer-marketed sub-model with different fans + accessory color kit, not just a paint job). 'Noctua NH-D15' (standard brown/beige) → variant=''. 'be quiet! Dark Rock Pro 5' → variant=''. 'Thermalright Peerless Assassin 120 SE WHITE ARGB' → variant='ARGB' only if ARGB is a distinct SKU not captured by another field; otherwise leave variant='' and put 'White' in manufacturer_color. The variant must be unique within a (manufacturer, series, water_cooled, manufacturer_color, radiator_size, fan_size) group; if every cooler in a group ends up with the same value, leave it empty.

manufacturer_color.description:
Manufacturer's official color name for this product variant.

Use the manufacturer's marketed colorway / finish name verbatim. Common cooler colorways: 'Black', 'White', 'Black / White', 'Silver', 'Brown / Beige' (Noctua's classic look), 'chromax.black' (Noctua's blacked-out accessories — but this is also commonly the variant for Noctua air coolers, see variant rules), 'Matte Black'. For multi-tone coolers (e.g., a black radiator with a white pump head), separate tones with ' / ' (e.g., 'Black / White'). Branded special / collab colorways DO belong here when the manufacturer markets them as a distinct colorway and the rest of the SKU is identical: 'Hatsune Miku Edition', 'Halo Edition'. Most stock coolers are 'Black'. Do NOT put radiator size ('240mm', '360mm'), fan size ('120mm', '140mm'), 'RGB', or family / model words like 'Kraken', 'Elite', 'Pro' in manufacturer_color — those belong in the series, dedicated fields, or variant. Color is purely the visual finish of the cooler / radiator / pump head.

last_manually_spec_verified_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the part specs were last manually verified by a human
